title: Rhys Dallows

{{Quote|Rhys, I'm counting on you making it through here. I know you can do this, so don't let me down.|[[Essara Till/Legends|Essara Till]], to Rhys Dallows|<ref name="Starfighter" />}}
'''Rhys Dallows''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] from [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. Trained under the ace pilot [[Essara Till/Legends|Essara Till]], he was assigned to [[Bravo Squadron/Legends|Bravo Flight]] briefly before the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]] by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]]. When Rhys began to develop feelings for Essara, she was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in only their first mission together by a band of [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] employed by the Trade Federation. Dallows himself barely survived the attack but swore to track down Essara's [[Unidentified mercenary leader|killer]] and have revenge. He then joined a motley crew consisting of the [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Vana Sage]], the [[Scavenger/Legends|salvager]] [[Reti]] and the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Nym/Legends|Nym]] in a vendetta against the Trade Federation. The four pilots headed to the occupied Naboo where they carried out several successful strikes against the Trade Federation. Rhys Dallows eventually joined up with Bravo Flight for the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|final battle]] of the conflict. Rhys was tasked to destroy the receiver stations of the Trade Federation [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|droid control ship]] but the mission turned into a disastrous ambush when the mercenary ship that had killed Essara appeared. Rhys eventually managed to destroy him and flew out just in time before the whole space station exploded from the inside. With the Trade Federation defeated, Rhys Dallows returned to the liberated Naboo as a hero. Decades later, Rhys Dallows joined the [[Naboo Resistance (Galactic Civil War)|Naboo Resistance]] against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

==Biography==
===Early life and early career===
{{Quote|I couldn't follow that order, Flight Leader, not when you were taking on that monstrosity by yourself.|Rhys Dallows, to Essara Till, during the battle at TFP-9|<ref name="TST" />}}
Rhys Dallows was born in the outskirts of [[Theed/Legends|Theed]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital city]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. He was raised by his mother, a schoolteacher, as his [[Reeve Dallows|father]], a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] went missing during a routine mission when Rhys was still a baby.<ref name="SPOSG" /><ref name="stats">{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=starfighter_stats|text="The Starfighter Trap" Character Statistics}}</ref>

Rhys had inherited his father's piloting talent and could manage well any vehicle, and his mother encouraged him. Despite his father's fate (believed to be captured by [[pirate/Legends|pirates]]), Rhys always dreamed to follow his steps, and protect innocent pilots from the dangers of space.<ref name="stats" />

As a young man, Rhys took a job at the [[Theed Municipal Spaceport]] as a [[technician]] (mechanic’s assistant) before joining the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Security Force]] as a volunteer.<ref name="stats" /> It was not long before his talent was discovered, bringing him to the [[Naboo Royal Space Fighter Corps/Legends|starfighter corps]] of the Security Forces.<ref name="stats" /><ref name="SPOSG" /> He was assigned to [[Echo Flight]], a [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of [[N-X Police Cruiser/Legends|N-X Police Cruisers]], as [[Echo Five]].<ref name="TST" /> Rhys showed an arrogant attitude, taking action whenever he felt his strong sense of justice was violated, to the point of disobeying orders from superiors he didn't respect. As such, he missed many assignments, until he reached his 22 years, older than most of his Flight.<ref name="stats" />

In [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]],<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> he participated in the disastrous [[Battle at TFP-9|battle]] at [[Station TFP-9]] under [[Essara Till/Legends|Essara Till]] as a [[Flight leader/Legends|flight leader]]. During the battle he made himself noticeable by his superiors of Bravo Flight, talking a lot over the radio, expressing zeal and commenting on their adversaries's stats, pointing out the superiority of their own fighters. When Captain [[Sorran]] demanded their surrender, Dallows angrily responded that they do not obey [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorists]]. When Till regrouped the flight, it was Dallows who reported the casualties, and when she decided to comply with the pirate's demands, Dallows was shocked. He disobeyed, and seeing that Till was making a run against one of the [[Hornet-class carrier|''Hornet''-class carriers]], he joined her; absorbing some fire from its point defense weapons, he added his firepower to hers to bring it down.<ref name="TST" />

For the remainder of the battle, he cooperated closely with Till, reporting to her the developments such as [[Harlaan]]'s death. Seeing that the traitor Melne, pursued by Till's [[torpedo/Legends|torpedoes]], was making a run for [[Velumina|his own carrier]], Dallows offered to cut him off and took formation with her. They made it between Melne and the carrier, when it launched a dozen [[missile/Legends|missiles]] against them, including Melne.<ref name="TST" />

Since Dallows was pivotal in the battle, he was among the three pilots (along with [[Evenyl Yob]] and [[Keela Egast]]) that Till recommended to [[Ric Olié/Legends|Ric Olié]] to join the prestigious [[Bravo Squadron/Legends|Bravo Flight]] to fill Dren's vacant position.<ref name="TST" /> Rhys was recruited by Till.<ref name="stats" />

[[File:SWS_NabTrainCan01.jpg|thumb|left|Rhys Dallows and Essara Till entering a training canyon in their N-1 starfighters.]]
Rhys Dallows was placed under the tutelage of Essara Till and trained to the [[N-1 starfighter/Legends|N-1 starfighter]]. He proved a quick learner even though he probably did not read the combat manual cover-to-cover. After passing Essara's tests in her favorite [[Naboo training canyon|training canyon]], he was picked by her to be her wingman in Bravo Flight as Bravo 10 in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. Rhys was also assigned a [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series astromech droid]] named [[Wrench (R2 unit)|Wrench]].<ref name="Starfighter" />

===Invasion of Naboo===
{{Dialogue|Dallows|We're planning on blowing up the Trade Federation droid factory.|Nym|You and whose army?|Sage|Yours.|attr=Rhys Dallows, [[Nym/Legends|Nym]], and [[Vana Sage]]|src=<ref name="Starfighter" />}}
For Rhys Dallows' first mission, Bravo Flight was to protect the [[J-type 327 Nubian royal starship/Legends|Naboo Royal Starship]] while [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Amidala]] attempted to negotiate with the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] about the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Blockade of Naboo]]. It was supposed to be an easy run, but an [[Attempted kidnapping of Queen Amidala|attack]] by several [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] flying [[Dagger-class starfighter|''Dagger''-class]], [[MorningStar]], and [[Dianoga-class assault starfighter|''Dianoga''-class]] starfighters forced the rookie space jockey to fight for his life. In the end the Queen escaped, but Rhys' N-1 starfighter was badly damaged and Essara was killed by a late mercenary arrival.<ref name="Starfighter" />

[[File:SWS_NabooSkirmish01.jpg|thumb|right|280px|Rhys Dallows, Nym, Vana Sage and Reti arrived at the occupied Naboo]]
Fortunately, Rhys was saved by a [[Toydarian/Legends|Toydarian]] [[Scavenger/Legends|salvager]] named [[Reti]], who took him to the base of mercenary [[Vana Sage]] for a serious overhaul of his fighter. After rescuing Vana from her own prison cell Rhys learned that the Trade Federation was definitely behind the attempt on the Queen's life, and that it was building a [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Droid Army]] from a massive [[Droid factory (Eos)|factory]] on [[Eos]]. With the aid of Vana, Reti, and the [[Feeorin/Legends|Feeorin]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] captain [[Nym/Legends|Nym]], the leader of the [[Lok Revenants/Legends|Lok Revenants]] and a major grudge against the Federation, he set out to [[Battle of Eos (Invasion of Naboo)|destroy]] the Eos factory. In addition to Dallows' N-1 starfighter, the three other pilots provided their own vessels for the incoming battles: Nym's [[Scurrg H-6 Bomber/Legends|Scurrg H-6 prototype bomber]] [[Havoc (starfighter)|''Havoc'']], Vana's [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] ''[[Guardian Mantis]]'', and Reti's [[Light freighter/Legends|light freighter]] ''[[Zoomer]]''. When they reached Eos the team split into two groups: Vana and Nym headed down to hit the foundries while Rhys and Reti lured away the planet's defense squadrons of [[Scarab-class starfighter|''Scarab''-class]] and [[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|''Vulture''-class droid starfighters]] with attacks on orbital satellites and Federation freighters. The attack was a success, but Rhys soon learned that the Trade Federation had launched its invasion of Naboo.<ref name="Starfighter" />

By the time they had arrived, Naboo had [[Capture of Theed/Legends|fallen]], but a [[Naboo Resistance|Resistance movement]] had already begun and Rhys was quick to join-up. Upon arriving in the [[Naboo system/Legends|Naboo system]], Rhys and his new allies [[Skirmish over Naboo|attacked]] a Trade Federation [[Convoy/Legends|convoy]] and successfully stole its cargo. Rhys then [[Mission in the Naboo canyon|escorted]] Reti and his load of stolen supplies to a [[Naboo Resistance base|Resistance base]], but a Trade Federation army ruined the party and Rhys had to use his N-1 as a ground attack craft inside the tight confines of the canyons—not something the N-1 was built to do—to save the base. After this he was assigned to lead a flight of N-1s providing air cover for a group of rescue boats [[Raid on the Solleu River|attempting]] to break-out Naboo citizens from Trade Federation concentration camps. Between the gunboats, turrets, droid starfighters, bombers, and a [[Trade Federation Dropship|dropship]] full of [[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|Armored Assault Tanks]], it was not easy, but Rhys nevertheless prevailed.<ref name="Starfighter" />

[[File:Droid_control_ship_explodes.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Rhys Dallows and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] escape the droid control ship just before it explodes.]]
After a quick [[Battle of the Naboo resistance base|appearance]] over the [[Naboo resistance base|main Resistance base]] to give Nym the breathing room he needed to demolish the huge Federation army attacking the place, Rhys joined-up with the remainder of Bravo Flight for a last-ditch assault on the [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|droid control ship]] [[Vuutun Palaa/Legends|''Vuutun Palaa'']]. It was a desperate [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|battle]], a few inexperienced Naboo pilots in ships designed for dogfighting against waves upon waves of computerized starfighters and the deadly array of weaponry aboard the enemy command ship. By concentrating on destroying the receiver dish stations needed to launch fighters, Bravo Flight had managed to gain a little room to maneuver when the same [[Unidentified mercenary leader|mercenary leader]] who had killed Essara Till appeared on the scene. After a brief battle, the mercenary ducked inside the enemy hangar when the shields were lowered to launch a lander. Bravo Flight followed, but only Rhys was able to get inside before the shields were raised again. Alone, in a tight space, and with all the fighters and tanks yet to embark training their guns on him along with a mercenary planning to finish him off, Rhys' chances were looking pretty slim. Amazingly, he not only survived, but managed to demolish a huge amount of war materiel, kill the mercenary to avenge Essara, and escape the exploding warship courtesy of a few well-placed shots to the vessel's portside power grid. The destruction of the droid control ship resulted in the complete deactivation of the Trade Federation Droid Army on Naboo and in a humiliating defeat for the Trade Federation.<ref name="Starfighter" />

[[File:Nym_farewell.jpg|thumb|right|280px|Rhys Dallows says farewell to Nym]]

Once the battle for Naboo was concluded, Rhys Dallows returned to his duties with the Royal Naboo Security Forces.<ref name="Starfighter" /> Nym earned a pardon from [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] for his previous crimes and was offered a position in the RSF,<ref name="Crossbones">[[Starfighter: Crossbones 1|''Starfighter: Crossbones'' 1]]</ref> as well as Vana Sage. Although Vana agreed to stay on Naboo, Nym soon left to deliver a shipment of supplies to his men. However, the Feeorin did stay long enough to tell Rhys that if he ever got tired of working in paradise, he was welcome to join the Lok Revenants.<ref name="Starfighter" />

===Galactic Civil War===
{{Quote|Our secret conference with sympathetic Naboo nobles is about to begin. Captain Dallows in Theed has sent one of his security officers to Dee'ja Peak as a guard for the meeting.|[[Pooja Naberrie/Legends|Pooja Naberrie]], to a Rebel spacer|<ref name="SWG:SPN" />}}
Rhys Dallows continued to serve the RSF during the next decades. After the [[Declaration of a New Order]], he became a sympathizer of the rebellion against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], eventually joining the [[Naboo Resistance (Galactic Civil War)|Naboo Resistance]] and at some point was promoted to the rank of [[Captain/Legends|captain]].<ref name="SWG:SPN" /> In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],<ref name="SWG_timeline">{{SWG_timeline}}</ref> during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Captain Dallows recommended a group of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] operatives to [[Pooja Naberrie/Legends|Pooja Naberrie]], the leader of the Naboo Resistance. Later, he dispatched Officer [[Turon Adala]], one of his subordinate among the RSF and a fellow resistance member, to Pooja Naberrie in the city of [[Dee'ja Peak/Legends|Dee'ja Peak]] in order to ensure security of a secret conference of the Naboo Resistance.<ref name="SWG:SPN" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:RhysDallowsSWG.png|200px|thumb|Rhys Dallows cut depiction from ''Star Wars Galaxies'']]
Rhys Dallows first appeared in "[[The Starfighter Trap]]," a short story published in the [[Star Wars Gamer 1|first issue]] of ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' in [[November]] [[2000]].<ref name="TST" /> The short story was an introduction to the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']] in which Rhys Dallows was one of the three main protagonists. In the game, Dallows was voiced by [[Darren Scott]].<ref name="Starfighter" />

Cut dialogue files in ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter]]'' reveal that Rhys Dallows and his wingmen from Bravo Flight were at one point going to join the space battle over Geonosis in the final mission "The Jedi Master", as part of Nym's reinforcements. Darren Scott would have reprised his role as well.<ref name="Jedi Starfighter">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter]]''</ref>

Rhys Dallows was mentioned in the [[2003]] video game ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Roleplaying game|massively multiplayer online role-playing game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:SPN" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> Rhys Dallows was intended to appear in the game as a [[Wikipedia:Non-player character|non-player character]] but was cut from the final game. Nevertheless, an emulated depiction pulled from this character's Interchange File Format reveals what he would have looked like if he was added to the game. Captain Rhys Dallows would have been present on Naboo as a preclude to additional quests given by Senator Pooja Naberrie. He was intended to propose four quests to Rebel players: kill a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]], destroy a warehouse, escort RSF Captain [[Meiro Wellightner]], and kill a [[Storm commando/Legends|storm commando]].<ref name="SWG">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=other|directory tree files and source data}}</ref> The existence of these quests was indirectly mentioned in the game by Pooja Naberrie.<ref name="SWG:SPN">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=static|Senator Pooja Naberrie|Naboo}}</ref>
